compiled_state.title: test-index-duplicates-check: auto-union on merge -> hook exits 1, requires two commit attempts
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
The test-index-duplicates-check pre-commit hook detects duplicate source-file keys in .test-index, auto-unions them in place, then exits 1 to signal 'file was modified, re-stage and retry'. This causes two problems:

1. The user must run `git add .test-index && git commit` twice — the first attempt always fails even though the fix was applied correctly.
2. When this happens mid-merge-to-main.sh (as it did in worktree-20260419-150435), the fixup commit lands AFTER the merge script has already run, requiring a full second merge-to-main.sh invocation.

## Root Cause
The hook exits 1 unconditionally after modifying the file. It should exit 0 when the auto-union was the only issue and the resulting file is now valid.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create a merge that introduces duplicate keys in .test-index
2. Stage .test-index and run git commit
3. Hook fires, auto-unions, exits 1 — commit blocked
4. Re-stage and commit again — now succeeds

## Expected Behavior
Hook auto-unions duplicates, stages the corrected file itself, and exits 0 so the commit proceeds in one pass.

## Actual Behavior
Hook exits 1 after auto-union, blocking the commit and requiring a manual re-stage + retry.
